pkg/pgxpoolobserver.purpose: Single-function OTel metrics registration for pgxpool.Pool, exposing 13 pool statistics as observable gauges/counters via a single RegisterCallback call. Call ObservePoolMetrics once per pool at startup; the registered callback polls pool.Stat() on each OTel collection cycle.

patterns.name: Single RegisterCallback for all metrics
patterns.description: Collect all metric.Observable instances into allMetrics and pass them to meter.RegisterCallback in one call. Never register separate callbacks per metric — OTel requires all observables used inside a callback to be declared in the same RegisterCallback call.
patterns.example: _, err = meter.RegisterCallback(func(_ context.Context, o metric.Observer) error { ... }, allMetrics...)

patterns.name: Append-then-register pattern
patterns.description: Each metric is created with meter.Int64ObservableCounter/Gauge, error-checked immediately (return on error), then appended to allMetrics before RegisterCallback. New metrics must follow this exact sequence.

patterns.name: additionalAttributes variadic for label injection
patterns.description: Callers pass extra attribute.KeyValue pairs via the variadic additionalAttributes parameter; every ObserveInt64 call appends them via metric.WithAttributes(additionalAttributes...).

patterns.name: Guard derived metrics against division by zero
patterns.description: avgAcquiredDurationMetric is only observed when acquireCount > 0. Replicate this guard for any future ratio or average metrics.

key_file_guides.role: Entire package. Exposes only ObservePoolMetrics(meter, pool, ...attributes). 13 metrics registered in a single function.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Each of the 13 meter.Int64Observable* calls is checked for error and returns immediately on failure. Adding a new metric requires: create → check error → append to allMetrics → observe inside callback.

anti_patterns: Calling ObservePoolMetrics more than once for the same pool/meter pair — duplicate metric registration will error.
Adding a new metric without appending it to allMetrics — it will never be observed in the callback.
Omitting the division-by-zero guard on derived ratio metrics — produces NaN or panic in the callback.

decisions.decision: Observable push model (RegisterCallback) instead of explicit Record calls
decisions.rationale: Pool stats are instantaneous gauge values; polling them only when the OTel collector pulls avoids background goroutines and unnecessary polling when no collector is active.

pkg/redis.purpose: Thin factory layer for go-redis/v9 client construction from a Viper-backed Config struct, with built-in OTel tracing and metrics instrumentation via redisotel. Supports standalone and Sentinel topologies; always enforces TLS 1.3 minimum when TLS is enabled.

patterns.name: Validate before NewClient
patterns.description: Config.Validate() uses errors.Join to accumulate field errors. Always call Validate() before constructing the client in Wire wiring or application startup.
patterns.example: if err := cfg.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err }

patterns.name: Functional options for OTel providers
patterns.description: TracerProvider and MeterProvider are injected via Option funcs (WithTracingProvider, WithMeterProvider). Nil providers are silently ignored; options are layered on top of the base Options struct.
patterns.example: client, err := redis.NewClient(redis.Options{Config: cfg}, redis.WithTracingProvider(tp), redis.WithMeterProvider(mp))

patterns.name: Configure sets Viper defaults with prefix
patterns.description: Call Configure(v, prefix) to register all Redis defaults under a namespaced Viper prefix. Never call viper.SetDefault inline in app config for Redis settings.

patterns.name: OTel instrumentation is always attempted
patterns.description: redisotel.InstrumentTracing and InstrumentMetrics are called unconditionally; when no provider is set the opts slice is empty and the calls are effectively no-ops. Do not short-circuit these calls.
patterns.example: if err := redisotel.InstrumentTracing(client, tracingOpts...); err != nil { return nil, err }

patterns.name: TLS minimum version is TLS 1.3
patterns.description: When TLS.Enabled is true, MinVersion is always set to tls.VersionTLS13. Do not override or relax this in callers.

key_file_guides.role: NewClient factory. Applies functional options, builds TLS config, constructs standalone or Sentinel client, instruments tracing and metrics.
key_file_guides.watch_for: redisotel.InstrumentTracing and InstrumentMetrics are called even when providers are nil (empty opts slice). Do not add nil-guard short-circuits — the unconditional call is intentional.

key_file_guides.role: Config struct, Validate, Configure (Viper defaults), and Config.NewClient convenience method.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Config.NewClient() does not accept OTel options. Use NewClient(Options{Config: c}, WithTracingProvider(...)) directly when telemetry is needed.

anti_patterns: Constructing go-redis client directly in application code — OTel instrumentation would be missing.
Calling Configure with an empty prefix — all keys would collide at the root Viper namespace.
Ignoring Config.Validate() — an empty Address produces a client that fails at first use, not at construction.
Relaxing TLS MinVersion below tls.VersionTLS13 — this is a deliberate security invariant.

decisions.decision: OTel instrumentation is always attempted, not gated on provider presence
decisions.rationale: redisotel calls are no-ops when no provider is passed, so always calling them ensures new callers automatically get observability when they wire providers, without conditional branching.

code_examples.scenario: Constructing a Redis client with OTel in Wire wiring

pkg/ref.purpose: Single-file utility providing IDOrKey — a discriminated union type that holds either a ULID-format ID or an arbitrary string key, used wherever domain lookups must accept both identifiers interchangeably without ambiguity.

patterns.name: ULID-based discrimination via ParseIDOrKey
patterns.description: ParseIDOrKey uses ulid.Parse to decide which field to populate: successful parse → ID set, parse error → Key set. Never set both fields manually.
patterns.example: r := ref.ParseIDOrKey(rawParam) // r.ID set if ULID, r.Key set otherwise

patterns.name: Validate before service call
patterns.description: Always call Validate() before passing an IDOrKey into a service layer. It returns an error if both fields are empty (the all-zero case).
patterns.example: if err := r.Validate(); err != nil { return err }

key_file_guides.role: Defines IDOrKey struct with GetIDs, GetKeys, Validate, and ParseIDOrKey. The entire package surface.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Validate only guards the all-empty case, not the both-set case. Callers must not set both ID and Key simultaneously — semantics are undefined and there is no runtime check.

anti_patterns: Setting both ID and Key on an IDOrKey struct — undefined semantics, no runtime guard.
Skipping Validate() before service calls — silently passes empty identifiers into domain logic.
Adding new identifier union types here — define domain-specific discriminated unions in the relevant domain package instead.

decisions.decision: Use ulid.Parse as the discriminator rather than a regex or length check
decisions.rationale: ULID has a canonical 26-char Crockford Base32 format; parse error cleanly distinguishes it from freeform key strings without false positives from length heuristics.

pkg/server.purpose: Shared HTTP server primitives — request attribute extraction, structured logging middleware, powered-by header middleware, and a MiddlewareFunc type alias — reused by both the v1 (openmeter/server) and v3 (api/v3/server) API servers with no business logic.

patterns.name: OTel semconv attribute keys
patterns.description: Use go.opentelemetry.io/otel/semconv/v1.27.0 key constants for all HTTP attribute names. Never hardcode string keys.

patterns.name: Chi middleware.LogFormatter / LogEntry interfaces
patterns.description: RequestLogger implements middleware.LogFormatter and RequestLoggerEntry implements middleware.LogEntry. Pass RequestLogger to middleware.RequestLogger() to integrate with Chi's request logging chain.

patterns.name: Middleware factory pattern
patterns.description: Every middleware is exposed as a NewXxxMiddleware() function returning func(http.Handler) http.Handler, matching Chi's middleware signature.

patterns.name: req_id depends on upstream RequestID middleware
patterns.description: GetRequestAttributes populates the req_id field only when middleware.RequestID is in the middleware chain upstream. Absent middleware → absent attribute with no error.
patterns.example: if reqID := middleware.GetReqID(ctx); reqID != "" { attrs["req_id"] = reqID }

key_file_guides.role: Returns OTel-keyed attribute map for a request. Requires middleware.RequestID upstream for req_id field.
key_file_guides.watch_for: req_id is silently absent if RequestID middleware is missing; this is not an error.

key_file_guides.role: Structured slog-based request logger satisfying Chi's middleware.LogFormatter interface.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Normal requests log at slog.LevelDebug; panics log at slog.LevelError. Do not raise normal requests to LevelInfo without considering log volume.

key_file_guides.role: Defines shared MiddlewareFunc type alias used across v1 and v3 server wiring.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must stay in sync with Chi's func(http.Handler) http.Handler signature. Never wrap in a struct.

key_file_guides.role: Injects X-Powered-By header on every response.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Header value 'OpenMeter by Kong, Inc.' is asserted in poweredby_test.go — changing it is a breaking change for clients that parse this header.

anti_patterns: Adding business logic or domain package imports — this package must remain a pure infrastructure utility.
Hardcoding OTel attribute key strings instead of using semconv constants.
Returning errors from middleware constructors — they should panic on bad config or return a no-op handler.
Registering GetRequestAttributes usage without middleware.RequestID in the chain — req_id will silently be missing.

decisions.decision: Log at DEBUG for normal requests, ERROR for panics
decisions.rationale: High-volume request logs would flood INFO level in production; DEBUG keeps them filterable without losing panic diagnostics.

pkg/set.purpose: Thread-safe generic set backed by map[T]struct{} with RWMutex; provides Add, Remove, AsSlice, IsEmpty, Subtract, and Union for any domain package needing concurrent set semantics over comparable types.

patterns.name: Construct via New[T](items...)
patterns.description: Always use New[T]() or New(item1, item2). Never construct Set{} directly — the content map will be nil and any write will panic.

patterns.name: RWMutex for read/write separation
patterns.description: Mutating methods (Add, Remove) acquire write locks; read methods (AsSlice, IsEmpty) acquire read locks. Subtract and Union acquire read locks on all input sets before building the result.
patterns.example:
s.mu.Lock(); defer s.mu.Unlock() // writes
s.mu.RLock(); defer s.mu.RUnlock() // reads

patterns.name: Package-level Subtract and Union functions
patterns.description: Subtract and Union are package-level functions, not methods, to support multi-set operations. Subtract(a, b) removes items in b from a — argument order matches lo.Difference(base, toRemove).

key_file_guides.role: Full set implementation. All operations are in this single file.
key_file_guides.watch_for: AsSlice returns items in random map-iteration order — never assume stability. The slice is a snapshot; future mutations are not reflected. Subtract(a, b) argument order: a is the base set, b items are removed from it.

anti_patterns: Constructing Set{} directly — nil content map panics on first write.
Assuming stable ordering from AsSlice — map iteration is random.
Holding the AsSlice result and expecting it to reflect future Add/Remove calls — it is a copy.
Using this package for large sets under high contention — RWMutex is optimized for small sets; sync.Map may be preferable for high-read-concurrency large sets.

decisions.decision: Use RWMutex with a plain map over sync.Map
decisions.rationale: RWMutex with map[T]struct{} is simpler and lower-overhead for the small sets typical in domain use; sync.Map optimizes for high-concurrency read-heavy workloads with minimal write contention.
